senses.definition: Relating to quizzes or the act of quizzing; characteristic of a quiz or being quiz-like.
examples: The teacher handed out a quizzatorial workbook to the class.
Her quizzatorial smile suggested she enjoyed testing others' knowledge.

senses.definition: Past tense of quiz; to question someone to test their knowledge or to obtain information in an interrogative way.
examples: The detective quizzed the suspect for hours.
We were quizzed on capital cities in geography class.

senses.definition: A person who takes quizzes; someone who is being quizzed.
examples: The quizzee struggled with the tricky questions.
As the quizzee, she answered confidently.

senses.definition: A person who creates, administers, or participates in quizzes; a person who quizzes others.
examples: The quizzer hosted a trivia night at the cafe.
She is a quick-witted quizzer who never misses a beat.

senses.definition: Collections or groups of quizzes; a place or collection containing many quizzes.
examples: The app offers several quizzeries for science and history.
We browsed the quizzeries to find a quick round.

senses.definition: Multiple people who quiz or take quizzes; plural of quizzer.
examples: The quizzer group met weekly; the quizzers enjoyed friendly competition.
Two quizzers raised their hands to answer.

senses.definition: A place or event centered around quizzes; a collection of quiz-related activities.
examples: We visited the quizzery in town for a fun Friday night.
The quizzery offered questions on science and literature.

senses.definition: Multiple short tests or questions used to measure knowledge or learning.
examples: We did three quizzes this week in history class.
The online platform offers weekly quizzes.

senses.definition: Showing mild confusion or disbelief; puzzled or questioning in a playful way.
examples: She gave me a quizzical look when I explained the joke.
His quizzical smile suggested he wasn’t sure what was happening.

senses.definition: The state or quality of being quizzical; the degree of puzzlement or curiosity.
examples: Her quizzicalities were evident as she examined the map.
The peculiar quizzicalities of the riddle amused everyone.

senses.definition: The quality of being quizzical; a curious or puzzled attitude.
examples: Her quizzicality showed in the raised eyebrow and tilt of the head.
The detective noted the suspect's quizzicality during questioning.

senses.definition: In a quizzical or puzzled way; showing curiosity or confusion.
examples: He looked at the map quizzically and shrugged.
She tilted her head quizzically, trying to understand.

senses.definition: The state or quality of being quizzical; the degree of puzzlement or curiosity.
examples: The quizzicalness of his expression suggested a hidden meaning.
A note of quizzicalness laced her reply.

senses.definition: The process of turning something into a quiz or making it quiz-like.
examples: The app uses quizzification to turn lessons into interactive games.
Quizzification helped make the topic more engaging.

senses.definition: Multiple instances or examples of turning content into quizzes.
examples: The course included several quizzifications to reinforce learning.
We saw many quizzifications across the modules.

senses.definition: Turned into a quiz; converted to be quiz-like.
examples: The lesson was quizzified to boost retention.
They quizzified the material to make revision easier.

senses.definition: Turns something into a quiz; makes it more quiz-like.
examples: The app quizzifies textbooks for study sessions.
They quizzifies the onboarding process to test understanding.

senses.definition: To turn into a quiz; to make something into a quiz format.
examples: We will quizzify the training module next week.
The platform can quizzify any article.

senses.definition: Act of turning something into a quiz; the process of making something quiz-like.
examples: Quizzifying the material helped students concentrate.
They are quizzifying several chapters for revision.

senses.definition: The quality of being dizzy or having a sensation of spinning; here extended metaphorically to refer to a state of disturbance or excitement.
examples: A brief quizziness swept through the crowd during the quiz.
The sudden quizziness of the event surprised everyone.

senses.definition: Plural form of quizziness; multiple instances of dizziness or spinning sensations.
examples: They reported several quizzinesses during the ride.
The festival had a few brief quizzinesses before the performance.

senses.definition: The act of asking questions to test knowledge; the process of quizzing.
examples: The teacher enjoyed quick quizzing rounds after each chapter.
We spent the afternoon with oral quizzing and discussion.

senses.definition: Present participle of quiz; to question someone to test their knowledge.
examples: The coach was quizzing the players on tactics.
We were quizzing the trainees about safety procedures.

senses.definition: In a quizzical or perplexing way; with a puzzled or curious tone.
examples: She looked quizzingly at the chart before answering.
He shrugged quizzingly, unsure of the next step.

senses.definition: Multiple instances of quizzing; rounds of questioning.
examples: The event included several quick quizzings.
Her quizzings varied in difficulty.

senses.definition: Having the qualities of a quiz; playful or teasing in a questioning way.
examples: His quizzish tone made the discussion light-hearted.
A quizzish remark sparked a round of laughter.
